natural_language: Seven blocks occupy the workspace. There's a pre-existing two-block tower: B sits on A, and A is on the table. The other five blocks — C, D, E, F, and G — are each placed individually on the table, not stacked on anything. All tops are exposed: B (top of the tower) plus each of the five loose blocks. The manipulator starts empty. Here's the catch: the existing B-on-A tower must not be disturbed at all. Leave it exactly where it is. Your only job is to build a second tower out of the five loose blocks. Stack them in this order from the ground up: C at the bottom, then D on C, then E on D, then F on E, and G at the very peak. So you end up with two towers: the original B-on-A, and the new G-F-E-D-C column. Do NOT touch A or B.
